From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Latvian athlete

Valērijs Žolnerovičs

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | (1985-04-19) April 19, 1985 (age 40) |
| Height | 1.80 m (5 ft 11 in) |
| Weight | 66 kg (146 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Country | [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") |
| Sport | Athletics |
| Event | [Marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") |

**Valērijs Žolnerovičs** (born April 19, 1985 in [Ventspils](/wiki/Ventspils "Ventspils")) is a [Latvian](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") [Olympic](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games") athlete. He is the current holder of the [Latvian record](/wiki/Latvian_records_in_athletics "Latvian records in athletics") both in [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") and [half marathon](/wiki/Half_marathon "Half marathon").

Trained by [Dainis Lodiņš](/w/index.php?title=Dainis_Lodi%C5%86%C5%A1&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dainis Lodiņš (page does not exist)"), Žolnerovičs finished the [3000m steeplechase](/wiki/3000m_steeplechase "3000m steeplechase") at the [2008 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2008_Summer_Olympics "2008 Summer Olympics") in [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing") with a time of 8:37.65 (32nd overall). In 2010 he won the half marathon distance of [Riga Marathon](/wiki/Riga_Marathon "Riga Marathon"), finishing after 1:05:40 and beating the event record, set by [Pavel Loskutov](/wiki/Pavel_Loskutov "Pavel Loskutov") in 2008, by 12 seconds. At the 2011 [Lisbon Half Marathon](/wiki/Lisbon_Half_Marathon "Lisbon Half Marathon") Žolnerovičs broke the [Latvian record](/wiki/Latvian_records_in_athletics "Latvian records in athletics") in half marathon, finishing after 1:04:43.

At the 2013 [Frankfurt Marathon](/wiki/Frankfurt_Marathon "Frankfurt Marathon") Žolnerovičs broke the Latvian record in marathon for the second time, finishing after 2:14:33.
